does: This paper introduces AVBench, a comprehensive automated evaluation benchmark specifically designed for audio-video generative models. The benchmark implements ten fine-grained evaluation dimensions covering visual quality, audio quality, and cross-modal consistency, with particular emphasis on human-centric scenarios involving speech and interactions. Rather than relying on generic multimodal language models, AVBench employs specialized evaluators trained through preference learning on large-scale supervision data, enabling more accurate and nuanced assessment of AV generation model capabilities than existing coarse-grained benchmarks.
connects: This work builds on the growing field of multimodal generative model evaluation, extending beyond single-modality assessment frameworks. It responds to limitations in existing AV generation benchmarks that rely on preset evaluations and generic LLMs for assessment. The paper connects to broader research on automated evaluation metrics for generative models, preference-based learning for evaluators, and human-centric evaluation design. It also engages with prior work on audio-video synchronization and multimodal consistency metrics, while advancing the specific challenge of evaluating human-related scenarios where subtle synchronization and interaction details matter.
enables: AVBench enables more rigorous development and comparison of audio-video generative models by providing standardized, human-aligned evaluation. The specialized evaluators trained via preference learning establish a foundation for future work on domain-specific assessment metrics that could extend to other multimodal generation tasks. This work opens directions for improving AV model training by providing fine-grained feedback on specific failure modes. It also facilitates research into better human-speech synchronization, cross-modal coherence, and interactive realism in generative models, while establishing methodological patterns for building specialized evaluators when generic metrics prove insufficient.
